transcript: As he faced the firing squad, Colonel Aureliano Buendía was to remember that distant afternoon when his father took him to discover ice. Over the next 18 months, those words would blossom into 100 Years of Solitude, a novel that would go on to bring Latin American literature to the forefront of the global imagination, earning García Márquez the 1982 Nobel Prize for Literature. What makes 100 Years of Solitude so remarkable?
